title: Trishna Saikia
text:
About me

Thanks for stopping by!
I am a final-year PhD researcher in Computer Science and Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Indore, working at the intersection of machine learning, computer vision, multimodal learning, and physiological computing.
My research focuses on developing robust AI systems for extracting physiological and behavioral information from videos and biosignals under real-world conditions. I am particularly interested in video-based physiological sensing, healthcare AI, temporal representation learning, multimodal fusion, and trustworthy machine learning.

title: Trishna Saikia
text:
Research profile


Building AI systems that understand human physiology from videos and biosignals.
During my PhD, I have worked on remote physiological sensing methods for estimating heart rate, blood pressure, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and pain-related responses using face videos, rPPG signals, PPG signals, and multimodal physiological data.
My work includes the design of temporal convolutional networks, transformer-based architectures, cross-modal knowledge distillation frameworks, multimodal fusion strategies, and self-supervised or weakly supervised learning methods for healthcare applications.


Remote physiological sensing from videos and biosignals


Machine learning, computer vision, and multimodal learning


Healthcare AI and physiological computing




My research has led to publications in venues including IEEE TIM, ACM TIST, Digital Signal Processing, Expert Systems with Applications, ICPR, ICMI, and FG. I have also contributed to the development of large-scale physiological video datasets for blood pressure, respiratory rate, and SpO₂ estimation.


PhD in Computer Science and Engineering, IIT Indore, expected 2026


Prime Minister Research Fellow, Government of India


India RISE Fellow, Johns Hopkins University, Gupta-Klinsky India Institute


Visiting Research Scholar, University of Turku, Finland

title: Trishna Saikia
text:
Research interests


Physiological Sensing
rPPG-based estimation of heart rate, blood pressure, respiratory rate, and oxygen saturation from face videos and biosignals.


Computer Vision
Video-based analysis of physiological and behavioral signals under motion, illumination, compression, and real-world variations.


Multimodal Learning
Fusion of visual, temporal, and physiological modalities for healthcare and affective computing applications.


Healthcare AI
Robust, trustworthy, and deployable AI systems for non-contact health monitoring and biomedical signal analysis.

title: Trishna Saikia
text:
Selected highlights


Publications
14 peer-reviewed publications in journals and conferences.


Datasets
Physiological video datasets covering heart rate, blood pressure, respiratory rate, and SpO₂ estimation.


Fellowship
Prime Minister Research Fellow, India RISE Fellow, and Erasmus+ Visiting Research Scholar.


Mentoring
Experience in teaching, mentoring, and interdisciplinary research collaboration.

title: Trishna Saikia
text:
I am a final-year PhD researcher in Computer Science and Engineering at IIT Indore. My research lies at the intersection of machine learning, computer vision, multimodal learning, and physiological computing.
My work focuses on extracting physiological and behavioral information from videos and biosignals for applications in healthcare, including heart rate, blood pressure, respiration, oxygen saturation, and pain analysis.
I am a Prime Minister Research Fellow (PMRF), an India RISE Fellow, and currently a Visiting Research Scholar at the University of Turku, Finland.
Learn more about me →

title: Teaching
text:
PMRF Guest Faculty
Department of Information Technology, Gauhati University Institute of Science and Technology (GUIST) January 2025 – June 2026
Delivered lectures for undergraduate students in the Department of Information Technology.
Courses Taught

System Programming
Web Technology
Python Programming

The lectures focused on both theoretical foundations and hands-on programming concepts, helping undergraduate students develop practical skills.

Department of Computer Science and Engineering, SGSITS Indore March 2023 – December 2024
Delivered lectures for undergraduate and postgraduate students in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering.
Courses Taught

Deep Learning

The sessions covered modern machine learning techniques, neural network architectures with practical demonstrations and case studies.

title: Teaching
text:
Teaching Assistantships

Machine Learning (CS403/603)
Indian Institute of Technology Indore
Served as a Teaching Assistant for the undergraduate and postgraduate Machine Learning course. Responsibilities included conducting tutorial sessions, assisting students with assignments and projects, holding doubt-clearing sessions, and supporting course evaluation activities.
Topics Covered

Supervised Learning
Regression and Classification
Decision Trees
Support Vector Machines
Neural Networks
Model Evaluation and Validation




Software Engineering (CS208/258)
Indian Institute of Technology Indore
Assisted in teaching Software Engineering concepts through tutorials, assignment evaluation, and student mentoring.
Topics Covered

Software Development Life Cycle
Requirement Engineering
UML and Software Design
Software Testing
Agile Development Methodologies




Pattern Recognition (CS412/612)
Indian Institute of Technology Indore
Supported teaching activities for Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning courses, including tutorials and practical problem-solving sessions.
Topics Covered

Feature Extraction
Statistical Pattern Recognition
Classification Algorithms
Clustering Techniques
Dimensionality Reduction

title: Teaching
text:
